name: David Murray
description: American jazz musician (tenor saxophone and bass clarinet)
abstract: David Keith Murray (born February 19, 1955) is an American jazz saxophonist and composer who performs mostly on tenor and bass clarinet. He has recorded prolifically for many record labels since the mid-1970s.

interviews.title: David Murray interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 18, 2003

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Steve Reich and Musicians with World Saxophone Quartet

quotes.text: Well the near future is to mix this album. I’m playing Carnegie Hall on the fourteenth with my quartet with strings so I’m using ten — this time I’m using ten string players from New York. I hope they execute as well as the Cubans. I’m going to do this gig in Seattle I have to waste my time on doing. I probably will do a recording with Taj Mahal and Ishmael Reed, another Gwo-Ka with Kip Hanrahan. I think that’s on the books for October. November and December I’ll just probably be doing some gigs with the Gwo- Ka Masters. This little period of time right in here I’m concentrating on getting my kid in to a decent college. We’ll see, maybe he’ll wind up here.
quotes.caption: David Murray's 2003 Carnegie Hall performance with strings
quotes.doc_title: David Murray interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 18, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: the album, Blues for Coltrane actually got you the, you were awarded the Grammy for best jazz instrumental performance and that album included David Murray, Pharaoh Sanders, Cecil McBee, and Roy Haynes
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Jang
about_quotes.evidence: And my sextet included David Murray on tenor saxophone and bass clarinet... David is, yeah, he’s just, I can’t say that — he’s just on an amazing high level. And just my brief experience of us working together just elevated me on a level.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Jang interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 17, 2003 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Special Edition which had Arthur Blythe and David Murray and Peter Warren in it. So yeah I liked the writing on there and the improvisations.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I went out and sat in with different people and met a lot of different people, sitting in with people like Jon Faddis or David Murray
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...I thought I was going to run into David Murray or James Newton... because I knew they were in town...
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: When Rufus joined it was Howard, Rufus, John Purcell and David Murray. I wrote for that group...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
